subject: Science
question_text: A marble tile would feel cold as compared to a wooden tile on a winter morning, because the marble tile
options: is a better conductor of heat than the wooden tile.
is polished while wooden tile is not polished.
reflects more heat than wooden tile.
is a poor conductor of heat than the wooden tile.
answer_key: is a better conductor of heat than the wooden tile.
answer_explanation: Marble is a good conductor of heat and conducts heat away from our body more quickly than wood.

subject: Science
question_text: A beggar wrapped himself with a few layers of newspaper on a cold winter night. This helped him to keep himself warm because
options: friction between the layers of newspaper produces heat.
air trapped between the layers of newspaper is a bad conductor of heat.
newspaper is a conductor of heat.
newspaper is at a higher temperature than the temperature of the surrounding.
answer_key: air trapped between the layers of newspaper is a bad conductor of heat.
answer_explanation: The layers of newspaper trap air, and since air is an insulator, it prevents body heat from escaping.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli and Boojho measured their body temperature. Paheli found her’s to be 98.6 °F and Boojho recorded 37°C. Which of the followng statement is true?
options: Paheli has a higher body temperature than Boojho.
Paheli has a lower body temperature than Boojho.
Both have normal body temperature.
Both are suffering from fever.
answer_key: Both have normal body temperature.
answer_explanation: Normal human body temperature is 37°C, which is equal to 98.6°F.

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho has three thermometers as shown in Figure 4.1. He wants to measure the temperature of his body and that of boiling water. Which thermometer (s) should he choose?
options: Thermometer (i) or (iii) for measuring body temperature and (ii) for measuring the temperature of boiling water.
Thermometer (i) for measuring temperature of both.
Thermometer (ii) for measuring temperature of both.
Thermometer (iii) for measuring temperature of both.
answer_key: Thermometer (i) or (iii) for measuring body temperature and (ii) for measuring the temperature of boiling water.
answer_explanation: Clinical thermometers (i) and (iii) have a small range suited for body temperature, while a laboratory thermometer (ii) has a wide range suited for boiling water.

answer_explanation: The bulb of the thermometer should be fully surrounded by the substance and should not touch the sides or bottom of the container.

question_text: Fig 4.3 (a–d) shows a student reading a doctor’s thermometer. Which of the figure indicates the correct method of reading temperature?
answer_explanation: The thermometer should be read by holding it horizontally and keeping the level of mercury at the line of sight.

subject: Science
question_text: Shopkeepers selling ice blocks usually cover them with jute sacks. Explain why.
answer_key: Jute sacks contain air trapped between their fibres. Air is a poor conductor of heat. Therefore, it prevents heat from outside from melting the ice block.
answer_explanation: Insulating properties of trapped air.
provenance_excerpt: Shopkeepers selling ice blocks usually cover them with jute sacks. Explain why.

subject: Science
question_text: A laboratory thermometer A is kept 7 cm away on the side of the flame while a similar thermometer B is kept 7 cm above the flame of a candle as shown in Figure 4.5. Which of the thermometers, A or B, will show a greater rise in temperature? Give reason for your answer.
answer_key: Thermometer B will show a greater rise in temperature. Hot air rises up by convection, so heat is transferred more efficiently upwards than sideways.
answer_explanation: Convection currents travel upwards.

subject: Science
question_text: To keep her soup warm Paheli wrapped the container in which it was kept with a woollen cloth. Can she apply the same method to keep a glass of cold drink cool? Give reason for your answer.
answer_key: Yes, she can. The woollen cloth traps air, which acts as an insulator and restricts heat transfer from outside to the cold drink, keeping it cool.
answer_explanation: Insulation works in both directions (preventing heat loss or heat gain).

subject: Science
question_text: In a mercury thermometer, the level of mercury rises when its bulb comes in contact with a hot object. What is the reason for this rise in the level of mercury?
answer_key: Mercury expands on heating. The expansion causes the level of mercury to rise in the capillary tube.
answer_explanation: Thermal expansion of liquids.

subject: Science
question_text: A circular metal loop is heated at point O as shown in Figure 4.6. (i) In which direction would heat flow in the loop? (ii) In which order the pins at points P, Q and R fixed with the help of wax fall if points O, P, Q and R are equidistant from each other?
answer_key: (i) Heat will flow in both directions from O (towards P and towards R). (ii) Pins P and R will fall first simultaneously, followed by Q.
answer_explanation: Heat flows from a hotter region to a colder region through conduction in metals.

question_text: In the arrangements A and B shown in Figure 4.7, pins P and Q are fixed to a metal loop and an iron rod with the help of wax. In which case are both the pins likely to fall at different times? Explain.
answer_key: In case A, pins P and Q will fall at different times. Heat reaches pin P faster as it travels through one arm, but takes longer to reach Q. In case B, heat travels linearly, reaching P first, then Q.
answer_explanation: Wait, the actual solution depends on Figure 4.7, but in an iron rod (case B) they fall sequentially, whereas in a loop it splits.

subject: Science
question_text: For setting curd, a small amount of curd is added to warm milk. The microbes present in the curd help in setting if the temperature of the mixture remains approximately between 35°C to 40°C. At places, where room temperature remains much below the range, setting of curd becomes difficult. Suggest a way to set curd in such a situation.
answer_key: The container with milk and curd mixture can be wrapped in a woollen cloth, kept in a thermal flask, or placed in a warm place to maintain the temperature.
answer_explanation: Using insulators to maintain warmth.

subject: Science
question_text: You may have noticed that a few sharp jerks are given to clinical thermometer before using it. Why is it done so?
answer_key: The jerks are given to bring the level of mercury down below 35°C (or 94°F) into the bulb. This ensures an accurate starting point for a new reading.
answer_explanation: Functionality of the kink in a clinical thermometer.
provenance_excerpt: You may have noticed that a few sharp jerks are given to clinical thermometer before using it. Why is it done so?

subject: Science
question_text: Why is it advised not to hold the thermometer by its bulb while reading it?
answer_key: If we hold the thermometer by the bulb, the heat from our hand will cause the mercury to expand, giving an incorrect higher reading.
answer_explanation: Body heat affects the thermometer bulb.
provenance_excerpt: Why is it advised not to hold the thermometer by its bulb while reading it?

subject: Science
question_text: At a camp site there are tents of two shades – one made with black fabric and the other with white fabric. Which one will you prefer for resting on a hot summer afternoon? Give reason for your choice. Would you like to prefer the same tent during winter?
answer_key: During summer, I would prefer the white tent because white reflects most of the heat and stays cooler. In winter, I would prefer the black tent because black absorbs more heat, keeping it warm inside.
answer_explanation: Absorption and reflection of heat by dark and light colours.

subject: Science
question_text: While constructing a house in a coastal area, in which direction should the windows preferably face and why?
answer_key: The windows should preferably face the sea. This is to allow the cool sea breeze to enter the house during the day, keeping it cool.
answer_explanation: Understanding sea breeze.

question_text: Observe the picture given as Figure 4.8. Water is being boiled in a pan of wide base. (i) Which position P or T will feel warmer? (ii) Fill up the boxes P and T to indicate the mode of flow of heat to the hand.
answer_key: (i) Position P will feel warmer. (ii) Box P: Convection. Box T: Radiation.
answer_explanation: Heat transfer depends on direction. Hot air rises rapidly by convection while sideways transfer is mainly by radiation.

subject: Science
question_text: Look at Figure 4.9. The length of wire PQ in case of A is equal to the diameter of the semicircle formed by the wire CDE, in case B. One pin is attached to each wire with the help of wax as shown in Figure 4.9. Which pin will fall first? Explain.
answer_key: The pin on wire PQ (case A) will fall first. Heat travels through conduction. The straight wire is shorter in path length compared to the curved wire (semi-circle arc is longer than the straight diameter), so heat reaches the pin faster in Case A.
answer_explanation: Length of the conduction path affects the time taken for heat transfer.
